rules.threat: A storage account that allows plaintext HTTP lets blob traffic and SAS tokens be intercepted on the wire.
rules.title: Storage account must require secure transfer (HTTPS only)
requiredActions: Set enable_https_traffic_only = true on the azurerm_storage_account so only TLS connections are accepted.

rules.threat: Allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 to a storage account exposes data to downgrade and known TLS weaknesses.
rules.title: Storage account must require TLS 1.2 minimum
requiredActions: Set min_tls_version = "TLS1_2" on the azurerm_storage_account.

rules.threat: Allowing public blob containers lets anonymous users enumerate and download objects.
rules.title: Storage account must disallow public blob access
requiredActions: Set allow_nested_items_to_be_public = false on the azurerm_storage_account.

rules.threat: Without purge protection, an attacker (or accident) can permanently delete the Key Vault and its secrets, destroying recovery options.
rules.title: Key Vault must enable purge protection
requiredActions: Set purge_protection_enabled = true on the azurerm_key_vault.

rules.threat: Without a soft-delete retention window, deleted secrets/keys cannot be recovered after a destructive action.
rules.title: Key Vault must set a soft-delete retention period
requiredActions: Set soft_delete_retention_days = 90 on the azurerm_key_vault.

rules.threat: An Azure SQL server with public network access enabled is reachable from the internet for brute force and exploitation.
rules.title: Azure SQL server must disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the azurerm_mssql_server and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: An AKS cluster without Kubernetes RBAC grants flat, unscoped access — any compromised credential is cluster-admin equivalent.
rules.title: AKS cluster must enable role-based access control
requiredActions: Set role_based_access_control_enabled = true on the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster.

rules.threat: Without infrastructure encryption, storage data has only a single encryption layer — defense-in-depth at rest is reduced.
rules.title: Storage account should enable infrastructure encryption
requiredActions: Set infrastructure_encryption_enabled = true on the azurerm_storage_account (set at creation time).

rules.threat: A PostgreSQL server without SSL enforcement accepts plaintext connections, exposing credentials and data on the wire.
rules.title: PostgreSQL server must enforce SSL
requiredActions: Set ssl_enforcement_enabled = true on the azurerm_postgresql_server.

rules.threat: A MySQL server without SSL enforcement accepts plaintext connections, exposing credentials and data on the wire.
rules.title: MySQL server must enforce SSL
requiredActions: Set ssl_enforcement_enabled = true on the azurerm_mysql_server.

rules.threat: Enabling the non-SSL Redis port lets cache traffic — often session tokens — be intercepted in plaintext.
rules.title: Redis cache must disable the non-SSL port
requiredActions: Set enable_non_ssl_port = false on the azurerm_redis_cache.

rules.threat: An App Service that accepts HTTP exposes session cookies and credentials to interception and downgrade.
rules.title: App Service must be HTTPS-only
requiredActions: Set https_only = true on the azurerm_app_service.

rules.threat: A Linux web app that accepts HTTP exposes session cookies and credentials to interception and downgrade.
rules.title: Linux web app must be HTTPS-only
requiredActions: Set https_only = true on the azurerm_linux_web_app.

rules.threat: A Function App that accepts HTTP exposes function keys and payloads to interception.
rules.title: Function App must be HTTPS-only
requiredActions: Set https_only = true on the azurerm_function_app.

rules.threat: A Key Vault with default-allow networking is reachable from any network — one leaked token away from secret exfiltration.
rules.title: Key Vault should restrict network access

remediate.snippet: Add network_acls { default_action = "Deny", bypass = "AzureServices", ip_rules = [...] } or a private endpoint. The allowed IPs/subnets must be chosen by a human.

requiredActions: Add network_acls with default_action = Deny (and a private endpoint) to the azurerm_key_vault.

rules.threat: An NSG rule with source '*' on admin ports (22/3389) exposes SSH/RDP to the entire internet for brute force.
rules.title: NSG rule must not allow admin ports from any source

remediate.snippet: Replace source_address_prefix = "*" with specific corporate CIDRs, or use Azure Bastion / JIT access for 22 and 3389. Auto-fix cannot choose a safe source.

requiredActions: Restrict source_address_prefix to known ranges on the azurerm_network_security_rule (especially for ports 22 and 3389).

rules.threat: A MariaDB server without SSL enforcement accepts plaintext connections, exposing credentials and data.
rules.title: MariaDB server must enforce SSL
requiredActions: Set ssl_enforcement_enabled = true on the azurerm_mariadb_server.

rules.threat: A Cosmos DB account with public network access is reachable from the internet for credential abuse and exfiltration.
rules.title: Cosmos DB account must disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the azurerm_cosmosdb_account and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: An App Service allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 exposes traffic to downgrade and known TLS weaknesses.
rules.title: App Service must require TLS 1.2 minimum
requiredActions: Set site_config { min_tls_version = "1.2" } on the azurerm_app_service.

rules.threat: A Linux web app allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 exposes traffic to downgrade and known TLS weaknesses.
rules.title: Linux web app must require TLS 1.2 minimum
requiredActions: Set site_config { minimum_tls_version = "1.2" } on the azurerm_linux_web_app.

rules.threat: A Linux VM that allows password authentication is exposed to SSH brute force and credential stuffing.
rules.title: Linux VM must disable password authentication
requiredActions: Set disable_password_authentication = true on the azurerm_linux_virtual_machine and use SSH keys.

rules.threat: Without Transparent Data Encryption, Azure SQL data files are unencrypted at rest.
rules.title: Azure SQL database must enable Transparent Data Encryption

remediate.snippet: TDE is on by default for new azurerm_mssql_database; confirm transparent_data_encryption_enabled = true and configure azurerm_mssql_server_transparent_data_encryption with a CMK if required.

requiredActions: Confirm Transparent Data Encryption is enabled (CMK if required) for the azurerm_mssql_database.

rules.threat: Without server auditing, malicious queries and data exfiltration against Azure SQL leave no trail.
rules.title: Azure SQL server should enable auditing

remediate.snippet: Add an azurerm_mssql_server_extended_auditing_policy for this server writing to a storage account / Log Analytics. The audit destination must be chosen.

requiredActions: Add an azurerm_mssql_server_extended_auditing_policy for the azurerm_mssql_server.

rules.threat: Without Azure Policy add-on, the cluster has no admission-time guardrails against insecure workloads.
rules.title: AKS cluster should enable the Azure Policy add-on
requiredActions: Set azure_policy_enabled = true on the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster.

rules.threat: AKS local accounts bypass Entra ID/RBAC — a leaked kubeconfig grants standing cluster access with no central revocation.
rules.title: AKS cluster should disable local accounts
requiredActions: Set local_account_disabled = true on the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster and use Entra ID integration.

rules.threat: Without blob soft delete, an overwrite or deletion (accidental or ransomware) is unrecoverable.
rules.title: Storage account should enable blob soft delete
requiredActions: Add blob_properties { delete_retention_policy { days = 7 } } to the azurerm_storage_account.

rules.threat: Allowing plain FTP deployment exposes credentials and content over an unencrypted channel.
rules.title: App Service should restrict FTP to FTPS
requiredActions: Set site_config { ftps_state = "FtpsOnly" } (or "Disabled") on the azurerm_app_service.

rules.threat: An Azure SQL server without an Entra ID administrator relies solely on SQL logins, weakening central identity control.
rules.title: Azure SQL server should have an Entra ID administrator

remediate.snippet: Add an azuread_administrator block (or azurerm_mssql_server_microsoft_support_auditing_policy) referencing a specific Entra ID principal/object_id. Auto-fix cannot choose the admin identity.

requiredActions: Configure an Entra ID administrator for the azurerm_mssql_server.

rules.threat: Vault access policies are coarse and hard to audit; RBAC gives scoped, centrally-managed, revocable access.
rules.title: Key Vault should use RBAC authorization

remediate.snippet: Set enable_rbac_authorization = true and migrate existing access_policy blocks to azurerm_role_assignment. Flipping this without migrating breaks access, so apply manually.

requiredActions: Migrate the azurerm_key_vault to enable_rbac_authorization = true and role assignments.

rules.threat: The ACR admin user is a shared static credential; if leaked it grants full push/pull, enabling image tampering.
rules.title: Container Registry must disable the admin user
requiredActions: Set admin_enabled = false on the azurerm_container_registry and use Entra ID / managed identity authentication.

rules.threat: A Container Registry with public network access can be reached for anonymous/credential-based pulls and pushes from the internet.
rules.title: Container Registry should disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the azurerm_container_registry and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: Without blob versioning, an overwrite (accidental or malicious) destroys the prior object with no rollback.
rules.title: Storage account should enable blob versioning
requiredActions: Add blob_properties { versioning_enabled = true } to the azurerm_storage_account.

rules.threat: Without container soft delete, a deleted container and all its blobs are unrecoverable.
rules.title: Storage account should enable container soft delete
requiredActions: Add blob_properties { container_delete_retention_policy { days = 7 } } to the azurerm_storage_account.

rules.threat: A Function App allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 exposes traffic to downgrade and known TLS weaknesses.
rules.title: Function App must require TLS 1.2 minimum
requiredActions: Set site_config { min_tls_version = "1.2" } on the azurerm_function_app.

rules.threat: A managed disk encrypted only with platform-managed keys offers less key control and revocation than a customer-managed key.
rules.title: Managed disk should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Set disk_encryption_set_id to an azurerm_disk_encryption_set backed by a Key Vault CMK. The DES resource must be supplied.

requiredActions: Attach a disk_encryption_set_id (CMK) to the azurerm_managed_disk.

rules.threat: A Key Vault secret with no expiration date stays valid forever if leaked, maximising the compromise window.
rules.title: Key Vault secrets/keys should set an expiration date

remediate.snippet: Set expiration_date (RFC3339) on the azurerm_key_vault_secret and rotate before expiry. Auto-fix cannot choose a meaningful expiry.

requiredActions: Set an expiration_date on the azurerm_key_vault_secret.

rules.threat: Remote debugging on an App Service exposes an attach point that can be abused to inspect or alter the running app.
rules.title: App Service must disable remote debugging
requiredActions: Set site_config { remote_debugging_enabled = false } on the azurerm_app_service.

rules.threat: The AKS HTTP application routing add-on creates a public DNS zone and ingress with weak controls, expanding exposure.
rules.title: AKS cluster must disable HTTP application routing
requiredActions: Set http_application_routing_enabled = false on the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster and use a managed ingress controller.

rules.threat: A Redis cache allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 exposes traffic to downgrade and known TLS weaknesses.
rules.title: Redis cache must require TLS 1.2 minimum
requiredActions: Set minimum_tls_version = "1.2" on the azurerm_redis_cache.

rules.threat: Shared-key access lets anyone with the account key bypass Entra ID/RBAC entirely — a leaked key is full data access.
rules.title: Storage account should disable shared key access
requiredActions: Set shared_access_key_enabled = false on the azurerm_storage_account and use Entra ID authentication (confirm all clients support it first).

rules.threat: A SQL firewall rule spanning 0.0.0.0 (Allow Azure Services) or 0.0.0.0-255.255.255.255 exposes the database broadly.
rules.title: Azure SQL firewall must not allow all addresses

remediate.snippet: Remove the 0.0.0.0 rule (Allow Azure Services) and the 0.0.0.0-255.255.255.255 rule; use specific IPs or a private endpoint. Auto-fix cannot choose a safe range.

requiredActions: Replace the broad azurerm_mssql_firewall_rule with specific source IPs or a private endpoint.

rules.threat: A Key Vault with public network access is reachable from the internet — one leaked token from secret exfiltration.
rules.title: Key Vault should disable public network access

remediate.snippet: Set public_network_access_enabled = false and add a private endpoint + network_acls. Confirm all clients reach the vault privately before applying.

requiredActions: Disable public network access on the azurerm_key_vault and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: A Service Bus namespace with public network access is reachable from the internet for credential abuse and message tampering.
rules.title: Service Bus namespace should disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the azurerm_servicebus_namespace and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: An Event Hub namespace with public network access is reachable from the internet for credential abuse and data interception.
rules.title: Event Hub namespace should disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the azurerm_eventhub_namespace and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: Cosmos DB local (key-based) auth bypasses Entra ID/RBAC — a leaked key is full, non-revocable data access.
rules.title: Cosmos DB should disable local (key) authentication
requiredActions: Set local_authentication_disabled = true on the azurerm_cosmosdb_account and use Entra ID RBAC (confirm clients support it first).

rules.threat: A Data Factory with public network access exposes its managed integration runtime and linked-service credentials.
rules.title: Data Factory should disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_enabled = false on the azurerm_data_factory and use a managed private endpoint.

rules.threat: An App Configuration store with public access exposes feature flags and configuration — sometimes containing secrets — to the internet.
rules.title: App Configuration should disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access = "Disabled" on the azurerm_app_configuration and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: A Defender for Cloud plan left on the Free tier means no threat detection for that resource type — attacks go unalerted.
rules.title: Defender for Cloud plans must be on the Standard tier
requiredActions: Set tier = "Standard" on the azurerm_security_center_subscription_pricing for each resource_type (VirtualMachines, SqlServers, StorageAccounts, KeyVaults, Containers, AppServices, etc.).

rules.threat: Without incoming client certificates, an App Service cannot enforce mutual TLS for service-to-service callers.
rules.title: App Service should require incoming client certificates
requiredActions: Set client_cert_enabled = true on the azurerm_app_service (for internal/mTLS apps).

rules.threat: Without a security contact, Defender for Cloud alerts (including active-attack notifications) are not delivered to anyone.
rules.title: Defender for Cloud should have a security contact

remediate.snippet: Set email (and enable alert_notifications/alerts_to_admins) on the azurerm_security_center_contact. The contact address must be chosen.

requiredActions: Configure a security contact email with alert notifications enabled.

rules.threat: A PostgreSQL Flexible Server allowing non-TLS connections exposes credentials and data on the wire.
rules.title: PostgreSQL Flexible Server must require TLS

remediate.snippet: Ensure the require_secure_transport server parameter is ON via azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_configuration { name = "require_secure_transport", value = "ON" }.

requiredActions: Set require_secure_transport = ON on the azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.

rules.threat: A Cognitive/AI Search service with public network access is reachable from the internet for query abuse and data exfiltration.
rules.title: Search service should disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the azurerm_search_service and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: An Event Hub namespace allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 exposes traffic to downgrade and known TLS weaknesses.
rules.title: Event Hub namespace must require TLS 1.2 minimum
requiredActions: Set minimum_tls_version = "1.2" on the azurerm_eventhub_namespace.

rules.threat: A Service Bus namespace allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 exposes traffic to downgrade and known TLS weaknesses.
rules.title: Service Bus namespace must require TLS 1.2 minimum
requiredActions: Set minimum_tls_version = "1.2" on the azurerm_servicebus_namespace.

rules.threat: A Cosmos DB account allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 exposes traffic to downgrade and known TLS weaknesses.
rules.title: Cosmos DB account must require TLS 1.2 minimum
requiredActions: Set minimal_tls_version = "Tls12" on the azurerm_cosmosdb_account.

rules.threat: An AKS API server reachable from any IP is exposed to credential stuffing and API exploitation.
rules.title: AKS API server should restrict authorized IP ranges

remediate.snippet: Add api_server_access_profile { authorized_ip_ranges = [<admin-cidrs>] } (or a private cluster). The allowed CIDRs must be chosen.

requiredActions: Restrict the AKS API server via authorized_ip_ranges or a private cluster.

rules.threat: An Application Gateway without WAF has no managed protection against L7 attacks (injection, bots).
rules.title: Application Gateway should attach a WAF policy

remediate.snippet: Attach a firewall_policy_id (azurerm_web_application_firewall_policy in Prevention mode) or a waf_configuration block to the azurerm_application_gateway with a WAF_v2 SKU.

requiredActions: Attach a WAF policy (Prevention mode) to the azurerm_application_gateway.

rules.threat: An Azure SQL server allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 exposes database connections to downgrade and known TLS weaknesses.
rules.title: Azure SQL server must require TLS 1.2 minimum
requiredActions: Set minimum_tls_version = "1.2" on the azurerm_mssql_server.

rules.threat: A Redis cache with public network access is reachable from the internet for brute force and abuse.
rules.title: Redis cache should disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the azurerm_redis_cache and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: Cross-tenant object replication can move data out to an attacker-controlled tenant.
rules.title: Storage account should disable cross-tenant replication
requiredActions: Set cross_tenant_replication_enabled = false on the azurerm_storage_account.

rules.threat: A PostgreSQL server with public network access is reachable from the internet for brute force and exploitation.
rules.title: PostgreSQL server should disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the azurerm_postgresql_server and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: A MySQL server with public network access is reachable from the internet for brute force and exploitation.
rules.title: MySQL server should disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the azurerm_mysql_server and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: Allowing SQL logins alongside Entra ID means a leaked SQL password bypasses centralized identity controls.
rules.title: Azure SQL server should enforce Entra ID-only authentication

remediate.snippet: Set azuread_administrator { azuread_authentication_only = true, ... } on the azurerm_mssql_server. Confirm all clients use Entra ID auth before enabling.

requiredActions: Enforce Entra ID-only auth (azuread_authentication_only = true) on the azurerm_mssql_server.

rules.threat: An AKS cluster without a network policy plugin allows unrestricted pod-to-pod traffic, so a single compromised pod can move laterally to every workload in the cluster.
rules.title: AKS cluster should enforce a network policy

remediate.snippet: Set network_profile { network_policy = "azure" } (or calico/cilium) on the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster. The network policy plugin can only be set at cluster creation.

requiredActions: Set network_policy in network_profile on the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster to restrict pod-to-pod traffic.

rules.threat: A PostgreSQL flexible server with public network access is directly reachable from the internet for brute force and CVE exploitation.
rules.title: PostgreSQL flexible server must disable public network access
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server and use a private endpoint or VNet integration.

rules.threat: An app accepting plaintext FTP exposes deployment credentials and source to interception on the wire.
rules.title: Linux web app must not allow plaintext FTP

remediate.snippet: Set site_config { ftps_state = "FtpsOnly" } (or "Disabled") on the azurerm_linux_web_app.

requiredActions: Set ftps_state = "FtpsOnly" or "Disabled" in site_config on the azurerm_linux_web_app.

rules.threat: Remote debugging left enabled on a production web app is an unauthenticated control channel into the running process.
rules.title: Linux web app must disable remote debugging

remediate.snippet: Set site_config { remote_debugging_enabled = false } on the azurerm_linux_web_app.

requiredActions: Set remote_debugging_enabled = false in site_config on the azurerm_linux_web_app.

rules.threat: A storage account whose network rules default to Allow is reachable from any network; only a default-Deny posture with explicit allowances actually restricts the blob/file endpoints.
rules.title: Storage account network rules must default to Deny

remediate.snippet: Add network_rules { default_action = "Deny", ... } (with explicit ip_rules / virtual_network_subnet_ids) to the azurerm_storage_account. Choosing the allowlist requires review.

requiredActions: Set network_rules.default_action = "Deny" on the azurerm_storage_account and add explicit allowances.

rules.threat: Without encryption-at-host, the temp disk and VM cache are written to the Azure host unencrypted, exposing data to a host-level compromise.
rules.title: Linux VM should enable encryption at host
requiredActions: Set encryption_at_host_enabled = true on the azurerm_linux_virtual_machine (the feature must be registered on the subscription).

rules.threat: An AKS cluster without a disk encryption set encrypts OS/data disks with platform-managed keys only, leaving no customer-controlled key revocation.
rules.title: AKS cluster should use a customer-managed disk encryption set

remediate.snippet: Set disk_encryption_set_id to an azurerm_disk_encryption_set (backed by a Key Vault key) on the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster.

requiredActions: Set disk_encryption_set_id (a customer-managed key) on the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster.

rules.threat: An AKS node pool without host encryption writes the node's temp disk and cache to the Azure host unencrypted, exposing workload data to a host-level compromise.
rules.title: AKS node pool should enable host encryption

remediate.snippet: Set default_node_pool { enable_host_encryption = true } on the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster (the EncryptionAtHost feature must be registered).

requiredActions: Set enable_host_encryption = true on the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster node pools.

rules.threat: A Key Vault key with no expiration date never rotates, so a key that is silently exfiltrated stays valid indefinitely.
rules.title: Key Vault key must set an expiration date

remediate.snippet: Set expiration_date (RFC3339) on the azurerm_key_vault_key and establish a rotation policy.

requiredActions: Set expiration_date on the azurerm_key_vault_key.

rules.threat: Without a SAS expiration policy, account SAS tokens can be minted with unbounded lifetimes; one leaked token is standing access to the storage account.
rules.title: Storage account should set a SAS expiration policy

remediate.snippet: Add sas_policy { expiration_period = "01.00:00:00" } to the azurerm_storage_account to bound SAS token lifetimes.

requiredActions: Add a sas_policy with a bounded expiration_period to the azurerm_storage_account.

rules.threat: A Service Bus namespace with local (SAS key) auth enabled lets a leaked shared access key bypass Entra ID/RBAC entirely.
rules.title: Service Bus namespace should disable local authentication
requiredActions: Set local_auth_enabled = false on the azurerm_servicebus_namespace (confirm clients use Entra ID).

rules.threat: A Bicep storage account allowing plaintext HTTP lets blob traffic and SAS tokens be intercepted.
rules.title: Bicep storage account must require HTTPS

remediate.snippet: Set properties: { supportsHttpsTrafficOnly: true } on the Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts resource.

requiredActions: Set supportsHttpsTrafficOnly: true on the storage account.

rules.threat: A Bicep storage account allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 exposes data to downgrade attacks.
rules.title: Bicep storage account must require TLS 1.2

remediate.snippet: Set properties: { minimumTlsVersion: 'TLS1_2' } on the Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts resource.

requiredActions: Set minimumTlsVersion: 'TLS1_2' on the storage account.

rules.threat: A Bicep SQL server with public network access is reachable from the internet.
rules.title: Bicep SQL server must disable public network access

remediate.snippet: Set properties: { publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' } on the Microsoft.Sql/servers resource and use a private endpoint.

requiredActions: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the SQL server.

rules.threat: A Bicep Key Vault without purge protection can be permanently deleted, destroying secrets and recovery options.
rules.title: Bicep Key Vault must enable purge protection

remediate.snippet: Set properties: { enablePurgeProtection: true } on the Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults resource.

requiredActions: Set enablePurgeProtection: true on the Key Vault.

rules.threat: A Bicep AKS cluster without RBAC grants flat, unscoped access — any leaked credential is cluster-admin.
rules.title: Bicep AKS cluster must enable RBAC

remediate.snippet: Set properties: { enableRBAC: true } on the Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters resource.

requiredActions: Set enableRBAC: true on the AKS cluster.

rules.threat: A Bicep Redis cache with the non-SSL port enabled lets cache traffic be intercepted in plaintext.
rules.title: Bicep Redis cache must disable the non-SSL port

remediate.snippet: Set properties: { enableNonSslPort: false } on the Microsoft.Cache/Redis resource.

requiredActions: Set enableNonSslPort: false on the Redis cache.

rules.threat: Public blob containers let anonymous users enumerate and download objects.
rules.title: Bicep storage must disallow public blob access

remediate.snippet: Set properties.allowBlobPublicAccess: false on the storage account.

requiredActions: Set properties.allowBlobPublicAccess: false on the storage account.

rules.threat: Single-layer encryption reduces defense-in-depth at rest.
rules.title: Bicep storage should enable infrastructure encryption

remediate.snippet: Set properties.encryption.requireInfrastructureEncryption: true.

requiredActions: Set properties.encryption.requireInfrastructureEncryption: true.

rules.threat: A leaked account key bypasses Entra ID/RBAC entirely.
rules.title: Bicep storage should disable shared key access

remediate.snippet: Set properties.allowSharedKeyAccess: false (confirm clients support Entra ID).

requiredActions: Set properties.allowSharedKeyAccess: false (confirm clients support Entra ID).

rules.threat: Cross-tenant replication can move data to an attacker-controlled tenant.
rules.title: Bicep storage should disable cross-tenant replication

remediate.snippet: Set properties.allowCrossTenantReplication: false.

requiredActions: Set properties.allowCrossTenantReplication: false.

rules.threat: Without a soft-delete window, deleted secrets cannot be recovered.
rules.title: Bicep Key Vault should set a soft-delete retention

remediate.snippet: Set properties.softDeleteRetentionInDays: 90 on the Key Vault.

requiredActions: Set properties.softDeleteRetentionInDays: 90 on the Key Vault.

rules.threat: A public Key Vault is one leaked token from secret exfiltration.
rules.title: Bicep Key Vault should disable public network access

remediate.snippet: Set properties.publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' and use a private endpoint.

requiredActions: Set properties.publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: Plaintext connections expose credentials and data on the wire.
rules.title: Bicep PostgreSQL server must enforce SSL

remediate.snippet: Set properties.sslEnforcement: 'Enabled' on the PostgreSQL server.

requiredActions: Set properties.sslEnforcement: 'Enabled' on the PostgreSQL server.

rules.threat: Plaintext connections expose credentials and data on the wire.
rules.title: Bicep MySQL server must enforce SSL

remediate.snippet: Set properties.sslEnforcement: 'Enabled' on the MySQL server.

requiredActions: Set properties.sslEnforcement: 'Enabled' on the MySQL server.

rules.threat: Plaintext connections expose credentials and data on the wire.
rules.title: Bicep MariaDB server must enforce SSL

remediate.snippet: Set properties.sslEnforcement: 'Enabled' on the MariaDB server.

requiredActions: Set properties.sslEnforcement: 'Enabled' on the MariaDB server.

rules.threat: HTTP exposes session cookies and credentials to interception.
rules.title: Bicep web/function app must be HTTPS-only

remediate.snippet: Set httpsOnly: true on the Microsoft.Web/sites resource.

requiredActions: Set httpsOnly: true on the Microsoft.Web/sites resource.

rules.threat: TLS 1.0/1.1 is exposed to downgrade and known weaknesses.
rules.title: Bicep web/function app must require TLS 1.2

remediate.snippet: Set properties.siteConfig.minTlsVersion: '1.2'.

requiredActions: Set properties.siteConfig.minTlsVersion: '1.2'.

rules.threat: A public Cosmos DB account is reachable from the internet.
rules.title: Bicep Cosmos DB must disable public network access

remediate.snippet: Set properties.publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' and use a private endpoint.

requiredActions: Set properties.publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' and use a private endpoint.

rules.threat: Key-based auth bypasses Entra ID/RBAC; a leaked key is full access.
rules.title: Bicep Cosmos DB should disable local auth

remediate.snippet: Set properties.disableLocalAuth: true (confirm clients support Entra ID).

requiredActions: Set properties.disableLocalAuth: true (confirm clients support Entra ID).

rules.title: Bicep VM should enable encryption at host

requiredActions: Set properties.securityProfile.encryptionAtHost: true on the Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines resource.

rules.title: Bicep storage account should set a SAS expiration policy

remediate.snippet: Set properties.sasPolicy.sasExpirationPeriod (e.g. '1.00:00:00') on the storage account.

requiredActions: Set properties.sasPolicy.sasExpirationPeriod on the storage account.

rules.threat: A Bicep App Configuration store with local auth enabled lets a leaked access key bypass Entra ID/RBAC.
rules.title: Bicep App Configuration should disable local auth

remediate.snippet: Set properties.disableLocalAuth: true on the configuration store (confirm clients use Entra ID).

requiredActions: Set properties.disableLocalAuth: true on the Microsoft.AppConfiguration/configurationStores resource.

rules.threat: A Bicep Key Vault whose network ACLs default to Allow is reachable from any network, one leaked token from secret exfiltration.
rules.title: Bicep Key Vault network ACLs must default to Deny

remediate.snippet: Set properties.networkAcls.defaultAction: 'Deny' and add explicit ipRules / virtualNetworkRules.

requiredActions: Set properties.networkAcls.defaultAction: 'Deny' on the Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults resource.

rules.threat: Without queue-service logging, an attacker who gains a storage key can read, enqueue, or delete messages and there is no audit trail to detect or reconstruct the abuse.
rules.title: Enable storage account queue logging

remediate.snippet: Add a queue_properties block with a logging sub-block enabling read, write and delete logging with a retention_policy_days value.

requiredActions: Add queue_properties { logging { delete = true read = true write = true version = "1.0" } } to the storage account.

rules.threat: Leaving SFTP enabled on a storage account exposes an additional credential-based remote file-transfer surface that attackers can brute-force or abuse to exfiltrate blob data.
rules.title: Disable SFTP on storage accounts
requiredActions: Set sftp_enabled = false unless SFTP is an explicitly reviewed requirement.

rules.threat: A storage account network rule set that defaults to Allow leaves blob, file, queue and table endpoints reachable from the public internet, enabling data theft if keys or SAS tokens leak.
rules.title: Default-deny on storage account network rules
requiredActions: Set default_action = "Deny" and explicitly allowlist required subnets and IP ranges.

rules.threat: Without ledger enabled, a privileged attacker or insider can tamper with database records and erase evidence of the change, defeating forensic and integrity guarantees.
rules.title: Enable ledger on SQL databases
requiredActions: Set ledger_enabled = true for tamper-evident integrity on sensitive databases.

rules.threat: Without a server security alert policy (Advanced Threat Protection), SQL injection, anomalous access and brute-force login attempts go undetected, giving attackers a quiet foothold.
rules.title: Configure SQL server threat detection alert policy

remediate.snippet: Add an azurerm_mssql_server_security_alert_policy with state = "Enabled" referencing the server.

requiredActions: Create an azurerm_mssql_server_security_alert_policy with state Enabled for the SQL server.

rules.threat: Without a vulnerability assessment, misconfigurations and excessive permissions in the SQL server persist unnoticed, widening the attack surface for data exfiltration.
rules.title: Enable SQL server vulnerability assessment

remediate.snippet: Add an azurerm_mssql_server_vulnerability_assessment referencing the server security alert policy with recurring scans enabled.

requiredActions: Create an azurerm_mssql_server_vulnerability_assessment with recurring scans for the SQL server.

rules.threat: With log_checkpoints off, the PostgreSQL server omits checkpoint records that aid in detecting tampering and reconstructing activity after a breach.
rules.title: Enable log_checkpoints on PostgreSQL

remediate.snippet: Set the log_checkpoints server parameter value to "on".

requiredActions: Set the log_checkpoints PostgreSQL configuration value to on.

rules.threat: With log_connections off, successful and failed authentication attempts are not recorded, hiding brute-force and credential-stuffing activity against the database.
rules.title: Enable log_connections on PostgreSQL

remediate.snippet: Set the log_connections server parameter value to "on".

requiredActions: Set the log_connections PostgreSQL configuration value to on.

rules.threat: With log_disconnections off, session termination events are not recorded, making it harder to correlate the duration and scope of an attacker's database session.
rules.title: Enable log_disconnections on PostgreSQL

remediate.snippet: Set the log_disconnections server parameter value to "on".

requiredActions: Set the log_disconnections PostgreSQL configuration value to on.

rules.threat: Without connection throttling, an attacker can mount unthrottled brute-force authentication attempts against the PostgreSQL server.
rules.title: Enable connection_throttling on PostgreSQL

remediate.snippet: Set the connection_throttling server parameter value to "on".

requiredActions: Set the connection_throttling PostgreSQL configuration value to on.

rules.threat: If require_secure_transport is OFF, MySQL flexible server accepts plaintext connections, letting an on-path attacker intercept credentials and query data.
rules.title: Require secure transport on MySQL flexible server

remediate.snippet: Set the require_secure_transport server parameter value to "ON".

requiredActions: Set the require_secure_transport MySQL flexible server parameter to ON.

rules.threat: Allowing TLS versions below 1.2 on MySQL lets an attacker downgrade the connection and exploit weak cipher suites to intercept traffic.
rules.title: Enforce TLS 1.2 on MySQL flexible server

remediate.snippet: Set the tls_version server parameter value to "TLSv1.2".

requiredActions: Set the tls_version MySQL parameter to TLSv1.2 only.

rules.threat: A Key Vault certificate without an expiry-bounded lifetime can outlive its safe rotation window, letting a stolen private key remain valid indefinitely.
rules.title: Set validity bounds on Key Vault certificates

remediate.snippet: Specify validity_in_months within the certificate_policy x509_certificate_properties block.

requiredActions: Set validity_in_months on the certificate policy to bound certificate lifetime.

rules.threat: An AKS cluster with a public API server exposes the Kubernetes control plane to internet-based credential and exploit attacks against the management endpoint.
rules.title: Enable private cluster on AKS
requiredActions: Set private_cluster_enabled = true so the API server is reachable only over the private network.

rules.threat: Without the OIDC issuer enabled, AKS cannot use workload identity federation, forcing long-lived static credentials that attackers can steal and reuse.
rules.title: Enable OIDC issuer on AKS
requiredActions: Set oidc_issuer_enabled = true to support federated workload identity.

rules.threat: Without workload identity, pods rely on shared node credentials or mounted secrets, expanding the blast radius when a single pod is compromised.
rules.title: Enable workload identity on AKS
requiredActions: Set workload_identity_enabled = true and assign scoped federated identities to pods.

rules.threat: Without the Key Vault secrets provider, secrets are stored in Kubernetes objects rather than a managed vault, making them easier for an attacker who reaches the cluster to harvest.
rules.title: Enable Key Vault secrets provider on AKS

remediate.snippet: Add a key_vault_secrets_provider block with secret_rotation_enabled = true.

requiredActions: Add a key_vault_secrets_provider block with secret_rotation_enabled = true.

rules.threat: Without Microsoft Defender integration, runtime threats and malicious container behavior in the cluster go undetected, letting an attacker operate without triggering alerts.
rules.title: Enable Microsoft Defender on AKS

remediate.snippet: Add a microsoft_defender block referencing a log_analytics_workspace_id.

requiredActions: Add a microsoft_defender block with a log_analytics_workspace_id.

rules.threat: Without the monitoring agent, cluster and container logs are not shipped to Log Analytics, so an attacker's actions in the cluster leave no centralized audit trail.
rules.title: Enable monitoring (OMS agent) on AKS

remediate.snippet: Add an oms_agent block referencing a log_analytics_workspace_id.

requiredActions: Add an oms_agent block with a log_analytics_workspace_id.

rules.threat: Without image cleaner, stale vulnerable container images accumulate on nodes, giving attackers more exploitable image versions to target.
rules.title: Enable image cleaner on AKS
requiredActions: Set image_cleaner_enabled = true to purge stale vulnerable images from nodes.

rules.threat: Anonymous pull on a container registry lets any unauthenticated party download images, potentially exposing proprietary code, secrets baked into layers, or internal service maps.
rules.title: Disable anonymous pull on container registry
requiredActions: Set anonymous_pull_enabled = false so registry pulls require authentication.

rules.threat: Without quarantine, images become pullable before being scanned, letting a vulnerable or malicious image be deployed before any security gate runs.
rules.title: Enable quarantine policy on container registry
requiredActions: Set quarantine_policy_enabled = true so images are scanned before they are pullable.

rules.threat: Without a retention policy, untagged manifests accumulate indefinitely, increasing the chance that an old vulnerable image is referenced and pulled.
rules.title: Configure retention policy on container registry

remediate.snippet: Add a retention_policy block with enabled = true and a days value.

requiredActions: Add a retention_policy block with enabled = true to purge stale untagged manifests.

rules.threat: A Linux web app without authentication configured exposes all application routes unauthenticated, letting attackers reach sensitive functionality directly.
rules.title: Enable authentication on Linux web app

remediate.snippet: Add an auth_settings_v2 (or auth_settings) block with enabled = true and a configured identity provider.

requiredActions: Add an auth_settings_v2 block enabling platform authentication for the web app.

rules.threat: Leaving HTTP/2 disabled forces clients onto older protocol versions and forgoes header compression integrity protections that mitigate certain downgrade and smuggling techniques.
rules.title: Enable HTTP/2 on Linux web app

remediate.snippet: Set http2_enabled = true inside the site_config block.

requiredActions: Set http2_enabled = true in the site_config block.

rules.threat: A Linux web app with public network access reachable bypasses private networking controls, exposing the app and its management surface to the internet.
rules.title: Disable public network access on Linux web app
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false and front the app with private endpoints.

rules.threat: A Windows web app that accepts HTTP lets an on-path attacker intercept session cookies and credentials in cleartext.
rules.title: Require HTTPS on Windows web app
requiredActions: Set https_only = true on the Windows web app.

rules.threat: Allowing TLS below 1.2 on a Windows web app permits protocol downgrade attacks against client connections.
rules.title: Enforce TLS 1.2 on Windows web app

remediate.snippet: Set minimum_tls_version = "1.2" inside the site_config block.

requiredActions: Set minimum_tls_version = "1.2" in the site_config block.

rules.threat: A Linux function app accepting HTTP exposes function keys and payloads to interception by an on-path attacker.
rules.title: Require HTTPS on Linux function app
requiredActions: Set https_only = true on the Linux function app.

rules.threat: A Linux function app allowing TLS below 1.2 permits downgrade attacks that weaken transport encryption.
rules.title: Enforce TLS 1.2 on Linux function app

remediate.snippet: Set minimum_tls_version = "1.2" in the site_config block.

requiredActions: Set minimum_tls_version = "1.2" in the site_config block.

rules.threat: Without a customer-managed key, Cosmos DB data is encrypted only with platform keys, so an attacker who compromises the platform key path or the account cannot be cut off by revoking a tenant-controlled key.
rules.title: Use customer-managed key for Cosmos DB
requiredActions: Set key_vault_key_id to a Key Vault key for customer-managed encryption.

rules.threat: Without virtual network filtering, the Cosmos DB account accepts connections from outside approved subnets, broadening the network reachable for data theft.
rules.title: Enable VNet filtering on Cosmos DB
requiredActions: Set is_virtual_network_filter_enabled = true and define virtual_network_rule entries.

rules.threat: Leaving access key metadata writes enabled lets a holder of a data key regenerate keys via the data plane, enabling an attacker to lock out legitimate users and persist access.
rules.title: Disable access key metadata writes on Cosmos DB
requiredActions: Set access_key_metadata_writes_enabled = false to prevent data-plane key regeneration.

rules.threat: An Event Grid topic with public network access exposes its event ingestion endpoint to the internet, enabling event injection or reconnaissance by unauthenticated parties.
rules.title: Disable public network access on Event Grid topic
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false and use private endpoints.

rules.threat: With local authentication enabled, an Event Grid topic accepts shared access keys that, if leaked, give an attacker full publish access bypassing Azure AD controls.
rules.title: Disable local authentication on Event Grid topic
requiredActions: Set local_auth_enabled = false and use Azure AD authentication.

rules.threat: An Event Grid domain with public network access exposes its event ingestion surface to the internet for injection or probing.
rules.title: Disable public network access on Event Grid domain
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the Event Grid domain.

rules.threat: Local auth on an Event Grid domain permits shared-key publishing that bypasses Azure AD and is exploitable if a key leaks.
rules.title: Disable local authentication on Event Grid domain
requiredActions: Set local_auth_enabled = false on the Event Grid domain.

rules.threat: Local auth on an Event Hub namespace permits SAS-key access that, if leaked, lets an attacker read or inject event streams without Azure AD controls.
rules.title: Disable local authentication on Event Hub namespace
requiredActions: Set local_authentication_enabled = false and use Azure AD for Event Hub access.

rules.threat: Local auth on a SignalR service permits access-key authentication that, if exposed, lets an attacker connect and relay messages bypassing Azure AD.
rules.title: Disable local authentication on SignalR service
requiredActions: Set local_auth_enabled = false on the SignalR service.

rules.threat: A SignalR service with public network access enabled exposes its real-time messaging endpoint to the internet for abuse and reconnaissance.
rules.title: Disable public network access on SignalR service
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the SignalR service.

rules.threat: Local auth on a Web PubSub service allows access-key authentication that an attacker can abuse if the key leaks, bypassing Azure AD.
rules.title: Disable local authentication on Web PubSub
requiredActions: Set local_auth_enabled = false on the Web PubSub service.

rules.threat: A flow log without a retention policy may discard network records before an investigation, erasing evidence of lateral movement or exfiltration.
rules.title: Set retention policy on network flow logs

remediate.snippet: Add a retention_policy block with enabled = true and days >= 90.

requiredActions: Add a retention_policy block with enabled = true and at least 90 days retention.

rules.threat: Without traffic analytics, raw flow logs are not correlated into actionable detections, so anomalous east-west traffic from an attacker goes unflagged.
rules.title: Enable traffic analytics on network flow logs

remediate.snippet: Add a traffic_analytics block with enabled = true referencing a Log Analytics workspace.

requiredActions: Add a traffic_analytics block with enabled = true and a workspace reference.

rules.threat: A virtual network without a DDoS protection plan is exposed to volumetric attacks that can take down public-facing services and mask intrusion attempts.
rules.title: Attach DDoS protection plan to virtual network

remediate.snippet: Add a ddos_protection_plan block with enable = true referencing a DDoS plan id.

requiredActions: Add a ddos_protection_plan block referencing an azurerm_network_ddos_protection_plan.

rules.threat: A managed disk with public network access permits export/import operations over the internet, which an attacker with a leaked SAS could use to copy disk contents.
rules.title: Disable public network access on managed disk
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the managed disk.

rules.threat: A managed disk with an open network access policy allows disk export to any network, enabling exfiltration of disk images by an attacker who can mint export SAS tokens.
rules.title: Restrict network access policy on managed disk
requiredActions: Set network_access_policy = "DenyAll" unless private-link export is required.

rules.threat: A Recovery Services vault without immutability lets an attacker who gains backup-operator rights delete or tamper with recovery points, defeating ransomware recovery.
rules.title: Enable immutability on Recovery Services vault
requiredActions: Set immutability = "Locked" on the Recovery Services vault.

rules.threat: Without infrastructure (double) encryption, vault data relies on a single encryption layer, reducing defense in depth against a key-compromise scenario.
rules.title: Enable infrastructure encryption on Recovery Services vault
requiredActions: Set infrastructure_encryption_enabled = true on the Recovery Services vault.

rules.threat: A Log Analytics workspace with short or unset retention may purge security logs before an incident is investigated, erasing the attacker's trail.
rules.title: Set retention on Log Analytics workspace
requiredActions: Set retention_in_days to at least 90 on the Log Analytics workspace.

rules.threat: A Log Analytics workspace allowing internet ingestion accepts data over public endpoints, which an attacker could abuse to inject misleading log data or probe the workspace.
rules.title: Disable internet ingestion on Log Analytics workspace
requiredActions: Set internet_ingestion_enabled = false and use private-link ingestion.

rules.threat: A Log Analytics workspace allowing internet query exposes the query endpoint publicly, broadening the surface for credential abuse to read sensitive logs.
rules.title: Disable internet query on Log Analytics workspace
requiredActions: Set internet_query_enabled = false and query over private link.

rules.threat: Without data exfiltration protection, a compromised Synapse workspace can route data to arbitrary external endpoints, enabling large-scale exfiltration.
rules.title: Enable data exfiltration protection on Synapse workspace
requiredActions: Set data_exfiltration_protection_enabled = true on the Synapse workspace.

rules.threat: Without a managed virtual network, Synapse Spark and integration runtimes share network paths that an attacker could traverse, weakening tenant isolation.
rules.title: Enable managed virtual network on Synapse workspace
requiredActions: Set managed_virtual_network_enabled = true on the Synapse workspace.

rules.threat: Allowing SQL local authentication on a Synapse workspace keeps a password-based attack surface that bypasses Azure AD conditional access and MFA.
rules.title: Require Azure AD-only auth on Synapse workspace

remediate.snippet: Set azuread_authentication_only = true inside the azure_devops_repo/aad_admin context; configure via the workspace aad_admin and disable SQL local auth.

requiredActions: Enable azuread_authentication_only on the Synapse workspace to disable SQL local auth.

rules.threat: A Databricks workspace without no-public-IP launches cluster nodes with public addresses, exposing them directly to internet-based attacks.
rules.title: Disable public IP on Databricks workspace

remediate.snippet: Set no_public_ip = true inside the custom_parameters block (requires VNet injection).

requiredActions: Set no_public_ip = true in custom_parameters and deploy with VNet injection.

rules.threat: A Machine Learning workspace with public network access exposes notebooks, models and datasets to internet-based attacks against the workspace endpoint.
rules.title: Disable public network access on ML workspace
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the ML workspace.

rules.threat: Without high business impact flag, an ML workspace logs additional diagnostic data that may include sensitive content, increasing exposure if logs are accessed by an attacker.
rules.title: Enable high business impact on ML workspace
requiredActions: Set high_business_impact = true to reduce sensitive data captured in diagnostics.

rules.threat: A Batch account with public network access exposes its job and pool management endpoints to the internet, broadening the surface for credential abuse.
rules.title: Disable public network access on Batch account
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the Batch account.

rules.threat: Local auth (shared access policies) on an IoT Hub permits SAS-key access that, if leaked, lets an attacker impersonate devices or read telemetry without Azure AD controls.
rules.title: Disable local authentication on IoT Hub
requiredActions: Set local_authentication_enabled = false on the IoT Hub.

rules.threat: An IoT Hub allowing TLS below 1.2 permits downgrade attacks against device-to-cloud connections, exposing telemetry and device credentials.
rules.title: Enforce TLS 1.2 on IoT Hub
requiredActions: Set min_tls_version = "1.2" on the IoT Hub.

rules.threat: An API Management instance allowing legacy TLS/SSL protocols lets attackers downgrade gateway connections and exploit weak ciphers to intercept API traffic.
rules.title: Disable legacy TLS protocols on API Management

remediate.snippet: Set the security block flags enable_frontend_ssl30/tls10/tls11 and backend equivalents to false.

requiredActions: Disable SSL3.0, TLS1.0 and TLS1.1 in the API Management security block.

rules.threat: An Application Gateway with an SSL policy permitting TLS 1.0/1.1 lets attackers force a protocol downgrade and exploit weak ciphers against backend-bound traffic.
rules.title: Enforce minimum TLS 1.2 on Application Gateway

remediate.snippet: Set min_protocol_version = "TLSv1_2" in the ssl_policy block.

requiredActions: Set min_protocol_version = "TLSv1_2" in the Application Gateway ssl_policy.

rules.threat: A Front Door security policy without a WAF leaves applications behind the CDN exposed to injection, bot, and L7 DDoS attacks at the edge.
rules.title: Associate WAF policy with Front Door

remediate.snippet: Reference a cdn_frontdoor_firewall_policy_id in the security_policies association block.

requiredActions: Associate a WAF firewall policy with the Front Door security policy.

rules.threat: Local auth (API keys) on a Cognitive Search service permits key-based access that, if leaked, lets an attacker read or modify the search index bypassing Azure AD.
rules.title: Disable local authentication on Search service
requiredActions: Set local_authentication_enabled = false and use Azure AD for Search access.

rules.threat: Without purge protection, an attacker who gains delete rights can permanently destroy an App Configuration store and its keys, causing irrecoverable outage.
rules.title: Enable purge protection on App Configuration
requiredActions: Set purge_protection_enabled = true on the App Configuration store.

rules.threat: Local auth on an App Configuration store permits access-key authentication that, if leaked, lets an attacker read configuration secrets bypassing Azure AD.
rules.title: Disable local authentication on App Configuration
requiredActions: Set local_auth_enabled = false on the App Configuration store.

rules.threat: Without a managed virtual network, Data Factory integration runtimes use shared public paths an attacker could leverage to reach connected data stores.
rules.title: Enable managed virtual network on Data Factory
requiredActions: Set virtual_network_enabled = true on the Data Factory Azure integration runtime.

rules.threat: A Windows VM without encryption at host leaves temp disks and cache unencrypted on the hypervisor host, exposing data to a host-level compromise.
rules.title: Enable encryption at host on Windows VM
requiredActions: Set encryption_at_host_enabled = true on the Windows VM.

rules.threat: A Windows VM not set to automatic patch assessment/installation accumulates unpatched OS vulnerabilities that attackers can exploit for initial access.
rules.title: Enable automatic patching on Windows VM
requiredActions: Set patch_mode = "AutomaticByPlatform" on the Windows VM.

rules.threat: A Linux VM not set to platform-managed patching accumulates unpatched packages that attackers can exploit for initial access or privilege escalation.
rules.title: Enable automatic patching on Linux VM
requiredActions: Set patch_mode = "AutomaticByPlatform" on the Linux VM.

rules.threat: A Windows VM directly associated with a public IP exposes RDP and other services to internet-wide brute-force and exploit attempts.
rules.title: Avoid direct public IP on Windows VM NICs

remediate.snippet: Remove public_ip_address_id from the ip_configuration and reach the VM via bastion or load balancer.

requiredActions: Remove direct public_ip_address_id association and use Azure Bastion for management.

rules.threat: With log_connections off on a PostgreSQL flexible server, authentication attempts are unrecorded, concealing brute-force activity.
rules.title: Enable log_connections on PostgreSQL flexible server

remediate.snippet: Set the log_connections flexible server parameter value to "on".

requiredActions: Set the log_connections PostgreSQL flexible server parameter to on.

rules.threat: With log_disconnections off on a PostgreSQL flexible server, session-end events are not recorded, hampering breach timeline reconstruction.
rules.title: Enable log_disconnections on PostgreSQL flexible server

remediate.snippet: Set the log_disconnections flexible server parameter value to "on".

requiredActions: Set the log_disconnections PostgreSQL flexible server parameter to on.

rules.threat: Without change feed, blob create/update/delete events are not captured, removing a forensic record that helps detect and reconstruct data tampering or ransomware.
rules.title: Enable blob change feed on storage account

remediate.snippet: Set change_feed_enabled = true inside the blob_properties block.

requiredActions: Set change_feed_enabled = true in the storage account blob_properties block.

rules.threat: A Data Explorer (Kusto) cluster without disk encryption leaves cached query data unencrypted on cluster disks, exposing it to host-level compromise.
rules.title: Enable disk encryption on Data Explorer cluster
requiredActions: Set disk_encryption_enabled = true on the Kusto cluster.

rules.threat: A Data Explorer cluster with public network access exposes its query endpoint to the internet for credential abuse and data exfiltration.
rules.title: Disable public network access on Data Explorer cluster
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the Kusto cluster.

rules.threat: Leaving SFTP enabled on a storage account exposes an extra credential-based file-transfer surface attackers can brute-force to exfiltrate blob data.
rules.title: Disable SFTP on storage accounts (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set isSftpEnabled: false in the storage account properties.

requiredActions: Set isSftpEnabled: false unless SFTP is an explicit reviewed requirement.

rules.threat: A storage account networkAcls defaulting to Allow leaves blob, file, queue and table endpoints internet-reachable, enabling theft if keys leak.
rules.title: Default-deny networkAcls on storage account (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set networkAcls.defaultAction: 'Deny' and allowlist required subnets/IPs.

requiredActions: Set networkAcls.defaultAction: 'Deny' and explicitly allowlist required networks.

rules.threat: Without Azure AD-only authentication, a SQL server keeps SQL logins active, preserving a password attack surface that bypasses conditional access and MFA.
rules.title: Require Azure AD-only auth on SQL server (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set administrators.azureADOnlyAuthentication: true on the SQL server.

requiredActions: Set azureADOnlyAuthentication: true in the SQL server administrators block.

rules.threat: An AKS cluster with a public API server exposes the Kubernetes control plane to internet credential and exploit attacks.
rules.title: Enable private cluster on AKS (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set apiServerAccessProfile.enablePrivateCluster: true.

requiredActions: Set enablePrivateCluster: true in apiServerAccessProfile.

rules.threat: Without the OIDC issuer, AKS cannot federate workload identities, forcing static credentials that attackers can steal and reuse.
rules.title: Enable OIDC issuer on AKS (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Add oidcIssuerProfile: { enabled: true } to the cluster properties.

requiredActions: Add oidcIssuerProfile with enabled: true to the AKS cluster.

rules.threat: Without Microsoft Defender, runtime threats in the AKS cluster go undetected, letting an attacker operate without alerts.
rules.title: Enable Microsoft Defender on AKS (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Add securityProfile.defender with a logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceId and securityMonitoring.enabled: true.

requiredActions: Add securityProfile.defender with securityMonitoring enabled to the AKS cluster.

rules.threat: A private AKS cluster that still publishes a public FQDN leaks control-plane addressing and can permit reachability that undermines the private design.
rules.title: Disable public FQDN on private AKS (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set apiServerAccessProfile.enablePrivateClusterPublicFQDN: false.

requiredActions: Set enablePrivateClusterPublicFQDN: false on the private AKS cluster.

rules.threat: Anonymous pull on a container registry lets unauthenticated parties download images, exposing proprietary code or baked-in secrets.
rules.title: Disable anonymous pull on ACR (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set anonymousPullEnabled: false in the registry properties.

requiredActions: Set anonymousPullEnabled: false so registry pulls require authentication.

rules.threat: Without a quarantine policy, images are pullable before scanning, allowing a vulnerable or malicious image to be deployed before any gate runs.
rules.title: Enable quarantine policy on ACR (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Add policies.quarantinePolicy.status: 'enabled' to the registry properties.

requiredActions: Add quarantinePolicy with status 'enabled' to the registry policies.

rules.threat: Leaving export enabled lets a holder of registry access push images out of the controlled boundary, aiding exfiltration of proprietary artifacts.
rules.title: Disable export policy on ACR (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set policies.exportPolicy.status: 'disabled' on the registry.

requiredActions: Set exportPolicy.status: 'disabled' on the container registry.

rules.threat: A web app with public network access reachable bypasses private networking controls, exposing the app to the internet.
rules.title: Disable public network access on web app (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' and use private endpoints.

requiredActions: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the web app.

rules.threat: Disabling HTTP/2 forces clients onto older protocol versions and forgoes integrity-protecting header handling that mitigates some downgrade techniques.
rules.title: Enable HTTP/2 on web app (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set siteConfig.http20Enabled: true on the web app.

requiredActions: Set http20Enabled: true in the web app siteConfig.

rules.threat: Without a customer-managed key, Cosmos DB uses only platform keys, preventing tenant-controlled key revocation to cut off a compromised account.
rules.title: Use customer-managed key for Cosmos DB (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set keyVaultKeyUri to a Key Vault key URI in the account properties.

requiredActions: Set keyVaultKeyUri to a customer-managed Key Vault key.

rules.threat: Without virtual network filtering, the Cosmos DB account accepts connections from outside approved subnets, broadening the exfiltration surface.
rules.title: Enable VNet filtering on Cosmos DB (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set isVirtualNetworkFilterEnabled: true and define virtualNetworkRules.

requiredActions: Set isVirtualNetworkFilterEnabled: true and add virtualNetworkRules.

rules.threat: Leaving key metadata writes enabled lets a data-key holder regenerate keys via the data plane, enabling lockout and persistence.
rules.title: Disable key metadata writes on Cosmos DB (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set disableKeyBasedMetadataWriteAccess: true in the account properties.

requiredActions: Set disableKeyBasedMetadataWriteAccess: true on the Cosmos DB account.

rules.threat: An Event Grid topic with public network access exposes its ingestion endpoint to internet event injection and reconnaissance.
rules.title: Disable public network access on Event Grid topic (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the Event Grid topic.

requiredActions: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the Event Grid topic.

rules.threat: Local auth on an Event Grid topic permits shared-key publishing that bypasses Azure AD and is abusable if a key leaks.
rules.title: Disable local auth on Event Grid topic (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the Event Grid topic.

requiredActions: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the Event Grid topic.

rules.threat: Local auth on an Event Grid domain permits shared-key publishing that bypasses Azure AD and is abusable if a key leaks.
rules.title: Disable local auth on Event Grid domain (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the Event Grid domain.

requiredActions: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the Event Grid domain.

rules.threat: Local auth on a SignalR service permits access-key authentication that an attacker can abuse if the key leaks, bypassing Azure AD.
rules.title: Disable local auth on SignalR (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the SignalR service.

requiredActions: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the SignalR service.

rules.threat: A SignalR service with public network access exposes its real-time messaging endpoint to internet abuse and reconnaissance.
rules.title: Disable public network access on SignalR (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the SignalR service.

requiredActions: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the SignalR service.

rules.threat: Local auth on a Web PubSub service allows access-key authentication that an attacker can abuse if the key leaks.
rules.title: Disable local auth on Web PubSub (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the Web PubSub service.

requiredActions: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the Web PubSub service.

rules.threat: Local auth on an IoT Hub permits SAS-key access that, if leaked, lets an attacker impersonate devices or read telemetry.
rules.title: Disable local auth on IoT Hub (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the IoT Hub.

requiredActions: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the IoT Hub.

rules.threat: An IoT Hub allowing TLS below 1.2 permits downgrade attacks against device-to-cloud connections.
rules.title: Enforce TLS 1.2 on IoT Hub (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set minTlsVersion: '1.2' on the IoT Hub.

requiredActions: Set minTlsVersion: '1.2' on the IoT Hub.

rules.threat: API-key auth on a Cognitive Search service permits key-based access that, if leaked, lets an attacker read or modify the index bypassing Azure AD.
rules.title: Disable local auth on Search service (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the Search service.

requiredActions: Set disableLocalAuth: true on the Search service.

rules.threat: Without purge protection, an attacker with delete rights can permanently destroy an App Configuration store and its keys, causing irrecoverable outage.
rules.title: Enable purge protection on App Configuration (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set enablePurgeProtection: true on the App Configuration store.

requiredActions: Set enablePurgeProtection: true on the App Configuration store.

rules.threat: A managed disk with an open network access policy allows disk export to any network, enabling exfiltration of disk images.
rules.title: Restrict network access policy on managed disk (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set networkAccessPolicy: 'DenyAll' unless private-link export is required.

requiredActions: Set networkAccessPolicy: 'DenyAll' on the managed disk.

rules.threat: A managed disk with public network access permits export/import over the internet, exploitable via a leaked SAS to copy disk contents.
rules.title: Disable public network access on managed disk (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the managed disk.

requiredActions: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the managed disk.

rules.threat: A VM without encryption at host leaves temp disks and cache unencrypted on the hypervisor host, exposing data to host-level compromise. (Distinct from the existing rule by targeting the security profile property.)
rules.title: Enable secure boot on VM (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set securityProfile.uefiSettings.secureBootEnabled: true on the VM.

requiredActions: Set secureBootEnabled: true in the VM uefiSettings.

rules.threat: A VM without vTPM cannot perform measured boot or attest its integrity, letting boot-level tampering go undetected.
rules.title: Enable vTPM on VM (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set securityProfile.uefiSettings.vTpmEnabled: true on the VM.

requiredActions: Set vTpmEnabled: true in the VM uefiSettings.

rules.threat: A Log Analytics workspace allowing internet ingestion accepts data over public endpoints, abusable to inject misleading logs or probe the workspace.
rules.title: Disable internet ingestion on Log Analytics (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set publicNetworkAccessForIngestion: 'Disabled' and use private link.

requiredActions: Set publicNetworkAccessForIngestion: 'Disabled' on the workspace.

rules.threat: A Log Analytics workspace allowing internet query exposes the query endpoint publicly, broadening the surface for credential abuse to read logs.
rules.title: Disable internet query on Log Analytics (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set publicNetworkAccessForQuery: 'Disabled' and query over private link.

requiredActions: Set publicNetworkAccessForQuery: 'Disabled' on the workspace.

rules.threat: An ML workspace with public network access exposes notebooks, models and datasets to internet-based attacks.
rules.title: Disable public network access on ML workspace (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the ML workspace.

requiredActions: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the ML workspace.

rules.threat: A Batch account with public network access exposes job and pool management endpoints to the internet for credential abuse.
rules.title: Disable public network access on Batch account (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the Batch account.

requiredActions: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the Batch account.

rules.threat: Without data exfiltration protection, a compromised Synapse workspace can route data to arbitrary external endpoints for large-scale exfiltration.
rules.title: Enable data exfiltration protection on Synapse (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set managedVirtualNetworkSettings.preventDataExfiltration: true on the Synapse workspace.

requiredActions: Set preventDataExfiltration: true in the Synapse managedVirtualNetworkSettings.

rules.threat: A Key Vault that bypasses network rules for Azure services widens the trusted path; an attacker abusing a trusted Azure service identity can reach the vault despite network restrictions.
rules.title: Set Key Vault network bypass to None (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set networkAcls.bypass: 'None' unless a specific trusted service requires access.

requiredActions: Set networkAcls.bypass: 'None' on the Key Vault unless explicitly required.

rules.threat: A Recovery Services vault without immutability lets an attacker with backup-operator rights delete or tamper with recovery points, defeating ransomware recovery.
rules.title: Enable immutability on Recovery Services vault (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set securitySettings.immutabilitySettings.state: 'Locked' on the vault.

requiredActions: Set immutabilitySettings.state: 'Locked' on the Recovery Services vault.

rules.threat: A Data Explorer (Kusto) cluster without disk encryption leaves cached query data unencrypted on cluster disks.
rules.title: Enable disk encryption on Data Explorer cluster (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set enableDiskEncryption: true on the Kusto cluster.

requiredActions: Set enableDiskEncryption: true on the Kusto cluster.

rules.threat: A Data Explorer cluster with public network access exposes its query endpoint to the internet for credential abuse and exfiltration.
rules.title: Disable public network access on Data Explorer cluster (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the Kusto cluster.

requiredActions: Set publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled' on the Kusto cluster.

rules.threat: Local auth on an Event Hub namespace permits SAS-key access that, if leaked, lets an attacker read or inject event streams. (Distinct property from existing disableLocalAuth coverage via require regex on a different namespace context.)
rules.title: Require zone redundancy disabled local auth note (Bicep)

remediate.snippet: Bound auto-inflate by setting a non-zero maximumThroughputUnits to prevent uncontrolled scaling abuse.

requiredActions: Set a bounded maximumThroughputUnits when isAutoInflateEnabled is true.

rules.threat: An attacker reaches the storage account directly over the public internet, bypassing private-endpoint controls and brute-forcing keys or SAS tokens.
rules.title: Disable public network access on storage accounts
requiredActions: Set public_network_access_enabled = false on the storage account and provision private endpoints for blob/file/queue/table.

rules.threat: Tools default to shared-key authentication instead of Azure AD identities, weakening auditability and enabling key-theft lateral movement.
rules.title: Default storage authentication to Azure AD (OAuth)
requiredActions: Set default_to_oauth_authentication = true on the storage account.

rules.threat: An attacker anonymously reads or lists blobs because a container is configured with blob or container public access.
rules.title: Keep storage containers private
requiredActions: Set container_access_type = "private" on the storage container.

rules.threat: An attacker or rogue admin permanently destroys keys in a managed HSM because purge protection is disabled, causing irrecoverable data loss.
rules.title: Enable purge protection on managed HSM
requiredActions: Set purge_protection_enabled = true on the managed HSM module.

rules.threat: An attacker reaches the managed HSM over the public internet because network ACLs default to Allow.
rules.title: Default-deny network access on managed HSM
requiredActions: Set network_acls.default_action = "Deny" on the managed HSM.

rules.threat: An on-path attacker downgrades the connection to a SQL managed instance to an obsolete TLS version and intercepts credentials and data.
rules.title: Require TLS 1.2 on SQL managed instances
requiredActions: Set minimum_tls_version = "1.2" on the SQL managed instance.

rules.threat: An attacker reaches the SQL managed instance directly over the internet because the public data endpoint is enabled.
rules.title: Disable public data endpoint on SQL managed instances
requiredActions: Set public_data_endpoint_enabled = false on the SQL managed instance.

rules.threat: An attacker with control of the Microsoft-managed key boundary, or a forced key disclosure, exposes data because TDE uses a service-managed key rather than a customer-managed key.

rules.threat: An attacker brute-forces or sprays PostgreSQL local passwords because password authentication is enabled alongside or instead of Azure AD.
rules.title: Disable password auth on PostgreSQL flexible servers
requiredActions: Set authentication.password_auth_enabled = false on the PostgreSQL flexible server.

rules.threat: Lack of Azure AD authentication forces reliance on shared database passwords, removing centralized identity governance and MFA enforcement.
rules.title: Enable Azure AD authentication on PostgreSQL flexible servers
requiredActions: Set authentication.active_directory_auth_enabled = true on the PostgreSQL flexible server.

rules.threat: An attacker's activity escapes forensic review because PostgreSQL log retention is too short or unset.
rules.title: Set PostgreSQL log retention days

remediate.snippet: Create an azurerm_postgresql_configuration with name = "log_retention_days" and an appropriate value (e.g. 7 or higher).

requiredActions: Add an azurerm_postgresql_configuration for log_retention_days with a value of at least 3.

rules.threat: Missing statement-duration logging hides slow or anomalous queries used in injection or exfiltration attacks.

remediate.snippet: Create an azurerm_postgresql_configuration with name = "log_duration" and value = "on".

requiredActions: Add an azurerm_postgresql_configuration for log_duration set to on.

rules.threat: An attacker's PostgreSQL flexible-server activity escapes review because log retention is too short.
rules.title: Set log retention on PostgreSQL flexible servers

requiredActions: Add an azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_configuration for logfiles.retention_days.

rules.threat: Forced key disclosure or a Microsoft-key-boundary compromise exposes MySQL data because encryption uses a service-managed key rather than a customer-managed key.
rules.title: Use a customer-managed key on MySQL flexible servers

remediate.snippet: Add a customer_managed_key block referencing a Key Vault key and the identity that can access it.

rules.threat: Ransomware or destructive actions leave no point-in-time recovery because Cosmos DB uses periodic rather than continuous backup.

rules.threat: Network ACL bypass for Azure services widens the trust boundary, allowing any Azure tenant resource to reach the account regardless of firewall rules.

requiredActions: Set network_acl_bypass_for_azure_services = false on the Cosmos DB account.

rules.threat: An attacker reaches the Redis cache from anywhere because a firewall rule spans the entire IPv4 range.

rules.threat: An attacker who gains a Kubernetes token escalates freely because authorization is not bound to Azure AD / Azure RBAC.

remediate.snippet: Within azure_active_directory_role_based_access_control set azure_rbac_enabled = true.

requiredActions: Set azure_active_directory_role_based_access_control.azure_rbac_enabled = true on the AKS cluster.

rules.threat: An attacker with management-plane access runs arbitrary commands inside the cluster via the AKS run-command feature, bypassing network controls.

rules.threat: A leaked API key grants full access because key-based local authentication is enabled instead of Azure AD.
